word_list.word: a fool and his money are soon parted
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ fˈuːl ænd hɪz mˈʌni ɑːɹ sˈuːn pˈɑːɹɾᵻd

senses.definition: used to imply that foolish, unwise, or careless people tend to lose their money quickly and easily, often due to poor financial decisions or scams

word_list.word: he that goes a borrowing, goes a sorrowing
word_list.pronunciation: hiː ðæt ɡoʊz ɐ bˈɔːɹoʊɪŋ ɡoʊz ɐ sˈɔːɹoʊɪŋ

senses.definition: used to warn that borrowing money may lead to negative consequences, such as financial difficulties and regret, and so people should be cautious and responsible in their financial dealings

word_list.word: interest on debt grows without rain
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɪntɹəst ˌɑːn dˈɛt ɡɹˈoʊz wɪðˌaʊt ɹˈeɪn

senses.definition: used to suggest that the longer a debt is left unpaid, the more it will grow and become more difficult to manage
used to discourage people from lending money to those who are not good at managing their own money, as they are likely to be bad at managing other people's money as well

examples.x: My friend learned the hard way that great spenders are bad lenders when they lent money to someone who had a reputation for overspending, and never got their money back.

word_list.word: debt is the worst (kind of|) poverty
word_list.pronunciation: dˈɛt ɪz ðə wˈɜːst kˈaɪnd ʌv pˈɑːvɚɾi

senses.definition: used to imply that being in debt can create financial instability, making it hard to achieve goals and escape poverty

examples.x: The student worked multiple jobs to pay off her student loans, acknowledging that debt is the worst poverty.

word_list.word: a fat kitchen makes a lean will
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ fˈæt kˈɪtʃən mˌeɪks ɐ lˈiːn wˈɪl

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of balance and self-discipline, suggesting that having an abundance of resources can lead to excess and lack of control

examples.x: John's spending was getting out of control, so his friend warned him that a fat kitchen makes a lean will.

word_list.word: beware of little expenses (, a small leak will sink a great ship|)
word_list.pronunciation: bɪwˈɛɹ ʌv lˈɪɾəl ɛkspˈɛnsᵻz ɐ smˈɔːl lˈiːk wɪl sˈɪŋk ɐ ɡɹˈeɪt ʃˈɪp

senses.definition: used to advise that small or seemingly insignificant expenses can add up over time and have a significant impact on one's finances

word_list.word: a man without money is a bow without an arrow
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ mˈæn wɪðˌaʊt mˈʌni ɪz ɐ bˈoʊ wɪðˌaʊt ɐn ˈæɹoʊ

senses.definition: used to imply that money is an essential tool or resource that is necessary for achieving goals and desires, and without it, a person is unable to take action or make progress towards their objectives

examples.x: When I lost my job and found myself struggling to make ends meet, I felt useless, understanding the truth behind the saying 'a man without money is a bow without an arrow.'

word_list.word: out of debt, out of danger

senses.definition: used to imply that being out of debt is a key factor in staying safe and secure financially
